Package org.apache.juneau.microservice.test

Class MicroserviceExtension

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.microservice.test.MicroserviceExtension
All Implemented Interfaces:
org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.AfterAllCallback, org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.BeforeAllCallback, org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.Extension, org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.ParameterResolver, org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.TestInstantiationAwareExtension
public class MicroserviceExtension extends Object implements org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.BeforeAllCallback, org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.AfterAllCallback, org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.ParameterResolver
JUnit 5 extension behind @MicroserviceTest: boots a whole Microservice (config + lifecycle + embedded Jetty on an ephemeral port) for the test class, with @TestBean mock-bean injection and convenience parameter resolution.
Lifecycle (per class)

The microservice is built + started once in beforeAll and stopped in afterAll. A fresh instance is booted per class and stopped cleanly — never reused, since Microservice restart is unsupported (the stopped flag is one-way, the bean store is closed, and each start() adds a JVM shutdown hook). The bound port is read from the live ServerConnector (never hard-coded), mirroring the established fixture pattern.

Mock-bean injection

@TestBean declarations are discovered via JuneauBeanStoreExtension.discoverOverrides(Object). Mode INJECT (the default) installs the overrides via Microservice.Builder.overridingBeanStore(...) before boot, so the service reads them from startup. Mode OVERLAY pushes the overrides onto the booted instance's bean store for the class duration and pops them in teardown.

Parameter resolution

Resolves RestClient (bound to the booted root URL), Microservice, WritableBeanStore, and the bound port (int / Integer) on test + lifecycle method parameters.

Since:
10.0.0
